profile_summary: Starting my career selling cars at 19 while in college, I quickly realized my knack for understanding people and building connections. With a Bachelor's in Marketing and a Master's in Ethical Strategic Leadership from the University of Central Missouri, I expected these degrees to open doors easily. Ironically, I found myself overqualified and facing more hurdles than anticipated, which taught me resilience and adaptability. My sales journey has been diverse, from winning national sales competitions in college to becoming the youngest territory manager in school photography sales. After relocating to Springfield, MO, I turned a struggling territory profitable within my first year, fueling my passion for transforming challenges into opportunities. In 2019, I transitioned to financial advising with Edward Jones, tripling the size of a small book of business within 11 months. The COVID-19 pandemic posed unforeseen challenges, particularly in maintaining client relationships. This led me to pivot to health insurance for a few years until I found my true calling in real estate and investment. My interest in real estate began in 2015 through the Bigger Pockets podcast and books. I bought my first duplex in 2016, living in one side while renting out the other. Despite a tumultuous 2018—marked by a move, job change, and divorce—I emerged stronger, selling my properties to meet financial obligations and focusing on rebuilding. Now, as the Vice President of Investor Relations at Ingram Capital, I bring my diverse experiences together. Partnering with Bryant Dawson, whom I met through Bigger Pockets, we focus on acquiring car washes, multifamily properties, shopping centers, and other cash-flowing businesses, ensuring robust returns for our investors. In addition to my role at Ingram Capital, I co-founded Contractors, Closers, and Connections (CCC) in Kansas City and Northwest Arkansas, creating meaningful, results-driven connections. Currently, I'm writing a book on using self-directed IRAs to invest in commercial real estate, aiming to empower others with the knowledge to achieve financial independence. Having started from scratch twice, I understand the value of perseverance and smart investing. I'm passionate about helping others achieve financial freedom through strategic investments and lasting partnerships.

Dependability and Accountability:

company_summary:
Mission
The Grate Products, LLC purpose is to protect people from the dangerous conditions that can exist in their basement, by providing our quality basement products and processes. Our goal is to make every home in the world healthier by helping our contractors provide their clients with products & services that promote a healthy basement environment.

History
In 2006, Steve and his team began work on his greatest invention, the GrateDrain System. The GrateDrain, along with GrateSump and GrateTrench, have changed the way basement drainage is being done. Steve pioneered the idea of closed drainage systems. He informed his local company in August 2006 that Pioneer Basement would only install closed drainage systems.

In late 2006, Grate Products, LLC was founded to distribute quality basement drainage products and train Authorized Contractors throughout the world as to properly installing them.

Today, Steve continues to help people make their basements healthy. Steve has served as President of the National Association of Waterproofing & Structural Repair Contractors (NAWSRC) in 2000-2001 and President of the Basement Health Association (BHA) in 2010-2011. He is currently serving as the Education Committee Chairman of the Basement Health Association. With his leadership, Steve is helping create a new and exciting industry which has the potential of improving the quality of life for millions of people through total basement health. Finally, all the service trades working in the basement environment will be talking and sharing to the benefit of all consumers.

Hestan Vineyards is a 41-acre vineyard located on Napa Valley's eastern slopes at the base of Okell Hill. Individually farmed blocks planted to all five Bordeaux varietals--Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc and Malbec--blanket the rolling hills overlooking the vineyard's 11-acre pond.

Consulting winemaker Mark Herold began working with the Hestan estate in 2002, creating his hallmark opulent wines with the 2002 Hestan Cabernet Sauvignon and the 2002 Meyer Cabernet Sauvignon. Both were a critical success.

In 2005, winemaker Jeff Gaffner began the Stephanie project to showcase all five of the Bordeaux varietals planted at the estate. He produces a Stephane Red Wine, (Bordeaux blend) , Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon from the property.

This harvest (2009) Thomas Rivers Brown is taking over production of the Hestan wines from Herold. Considered one of the rising stars of Napa Valley, Thomas made history as the youngest winemaker to receive two perfect 100 point scores from Robert Parker.

Hestan Vineyards owner Stanley Cheng notes, "We feel very excited and privileged to have winemakers of such accomplishment interpret our vineyard's terroir in such wonderfully expressive styles. We think our entire portfolio of wines--Hestan, Meyer and Stephanie--will continue to improve with each vintage."

company_summary:
Started in 2006, Advanced Component Services (ACS) is a contract manufacturer, commercial component distributor, e-commerce third party seller and, since 2014, an Amazon Vendor. ACS operates its main office and distribution center in the Seattle area and has support offices and warehouses in China and England.

Commercial Division

From its inception, ACS has operated as a contract manufacturer and commercial distributor primarily focused on developing and selling components used in commercial food service equipment, beverage equipment and lighting.

ACS has manufactured over 4,000 different products for its commercial customers. We directly or indirectly supply components to companies operating supermarkets, c-stores, bakeries, coffee shops, restaurants, office coffee service businesses and other types of establishments.

In addition to the products manufactured by our company, we maintain a large inventory of popular components from companies such as Robertshaw, Parker, Southco, Oetiker and several other fully stocked lines.

ACS is constantly looking for new products to manufacture for our existing and new commercial customers.

E-Commerce Division

In 2014, ACS launched an e-commerce division as part of a strategic initiative to create new revenue streams for its products. The e-commerce division has been very successful and has generated triple-digit growth since its creation. At the end of its fourth year, the e-commerce division was actively marketing and selling tens of thousands of products through a flow of up to 20,000 orders each month.

To support its e-commerce division’s growth, ACS is actively seeking new partnerships with component manufacturers and authorized distribution companies to purchase as many new products as possible to add to its product offering.

The e-commerce division’s products are sold through all the major marketplaces and standalone websites.

Static-control flooring is a tiny island in the giant sea of commercial flooring. Yet it’s the least understood, most technically challenging part of the flooring world. At StaticWorx, I collaborate closely with architects, designers, and contractors, translating complex technical details into real-world solutions that satisfy mutual objectives. If you’re looking for a partner who listens, genuinely cares, and will always do right by you, let’s connect. 

StaticWorx is responsible for sharing most of the publicly available static-control flooring learning content, the below article is a subject the design community has found most interesting.

company_summary:
StaticWorx provides high-performance static-control flooring systems engineered for mission-critical environments. Our GroundSafe® ESD flooring platforms prevent random electrostatic discharge (ESD) events in electronics manufacturing, SMT facilities, cleanrooms, labs, data centers, and any operation working with sensitive or high-speed electronic components.

Every solution begins with an assessment of your environment, risks, and performance thresholds. From there, we design a complete system that includes the optimal flooring material along with the correct installation method, grounding strategy, footwear protocols, seating, mobile carts, and other elements that influence charge generation and dissipation.

Our portfolio supports a wide range of technical and operational requirements:
– ShadowFX® SDT carpet tile
– AmeriWorx™ conductive and static-dissipative vinyl tile
– Eclipse™ EC and Eclipse Plus™ conductive rubber
– GroundWorx™ ESD epoxy and coatings
– GroundLock™ Extreme interlocking tile

Our agile team of ESD-control specialists supports architects, designers, engineers, contractors, and facilities managers with deep technical knowledge and real-world insight. We collaborate closely with teams in electronics manufacturing, aerospace, cleanrooms, government, healthcare, and advanced technology to ensure alignment with ANSI/ESD S20.20 and operational requirements.

All StaticWorx GroundSafe® floors are backed by a lifetime warranty for static control. After installation, we continue to support clients through testing, verification, and system optimization.

With StaticWorx, you gain a partner committed to the long-term performance of your ESD flooring system and the reliability of your operations.

Hestan Vineyards is a 41-acre vineyard located on Napa Valley's eastern slopes at the base of Okell Hill. Individually farmed blocks planted to all five Bordeaux varietals--Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc and Malbec--blanket the rolling hills overlooking the vineyard's 11-acre pond.

Consulting winemaker Mark Herold began working with the Hestan estate in 2002, creating his hallmark opulent wines with the 2002 Hestan Cabernet Sauvignon and the 2002 Meyer Cabernet Sauvignon. Both were a critical success.

In 2005, winemaker Jeff Gaffner began the Stephanie project to showcase all five of the Bordeaux varietals planted at the estate. He produces a Stephane Red Wine, (Bordeaux blend) , Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon from the property.

This harvest (2009) Thomas Rivers Brown is taking over production of the Hestan wines from Herold. Considered one of the rising stars of Napa Valley, Thomas made history as the youngest winemaker to receive two perfect 100 point scores from Robert Parker.

Hestan Vineyards owner Stanley Cheng notes, "We feel very excited and privileged to have winemakers of such accomplishment interpret our vineyard's terroir in such wonderfully expressive styles. We think our entire portfolio of wines--Hestan, Meyer and Stephanie--will continue to improve with each vintage."

company_summary:
StaticWorx provides high-performance static-control flooring systems engineered for mission-critical environments. Our GroundSafe® ESD flooring platforms prevent random electrostatic discharge (ESD) events in electronics manufacturing, SMT facilities, cleanrooms, labs, data centers, and any operation working with sensitive or high-speed electronic components.

Every solution begins with an assessment of your environment, risks, and performance thresholds. From there, we design a complete system that includes the optimal flooring material along with the correct installation method, grounding strategy, footwear protocols, seating, mobile carts, and other elements that influence charge generation and dissipation.

Our portfolio supports a wide range of technical and operational requirements:
– ShadowFX® SDT carpet tile
– AmeriWorx™ conductive and static-dissipative vinyl tile
– Eclipse™ EC and Eclipse Plus™ conductive rubber
– GroundWorx™ ESD epoxy and coatings
– GroundLock™ Extreme interlocking tile

Our agile team of ESD-control specialists supports architects, designers, engineers, contractors, and facilities managers with deep technical knowledge and real-world insight. We collaborate closely with teams in electronics manufacturing, aerospace, cleanrooms, government, healthcare, and advanced technology to ensure alignment with ANSI/ESD S20.20 and operational requirements.

All StaticWorx GroundSafe® floors are backed by a lifetime warranty for static control. After installation, we continue to support clients through testing, verification, and system optimization.

With StaticWorx, you gain a partner committed to the long-term performance of your ESD flooring system and the reliability of your operations.

Redux, a patented drying technology that removes 100% of the liquid from wet electronics if you encounter a spill, can save you time, money, and salvage your data.

Founded by Reuben Zielinski and Joel Trusty in Indianapolis in 2013, Redux uses their patented technique to remove moisture from liquid-damaged cell phones, cameras, tablets, MP3 players and other devices, and returns them to working condition.

The idea for Redux came when Trusty’s wife subjected her cell phone to a full cycle in the washing machine and was devastated that not only was the phone ruined, but all the data, contact information, photos and videos were lost. With a background in manufacturing, Trusty thought of a concept for how to save his wife’s phone and knew that his friend and neighbor, Zielinski, an ex-IBM employee, had the equipment to put it into practice. Together, they fixed the phone, and within one week created the first Redux model.

Currently, Redux is available at over 2000 wireless retail stores throughout the U.S. For more information, visit the Redux website.

company_summary:
Mission
The Grate Products, LLC purpose is to protect people from the dangerous conditions that can exist in their basement, by providing our quality basement products and processes. Our goal is to make every home in the world healthier by helping our contractors provide their clients with products & services that promote a healthy basement environment.

History
In 2006, Steve and his team began work on his greatest invention, the GrateDrain System. The GrateDrain, along with GrateSump and GrateTrench, have changed the way basement drainage is being done. Steve pioneered the idea of closed drainage systems. He informed his local company in August 2006 that Pioneer Basement would only install closed drainage systems.

In late 2006, Grate Products, LLC was founded to distribute quality basement drainage products and train Authorized Contractors throughout the world as to properly installing them.

Today, Steve continues to help people make their basements healthy. Steve has served as President of the National Association of Waterproofing & Structural Repair Contractors (NAWSRC) in 2000-2001 and President of the Basement Health Association (BHA) in 2010-2011. He is currently serving as the Education Committee Chairman of the Basement Health Association. With his leadership, Steve is helping create a new and exciting industry which has the potential of improving the quality of life for millions of people through total basement health. Finally, all the service trades working in the basement environment will be talking and sharing to the benefit of all consumers.

Redux, a patented drying technology that removes 100% of the liquid from wet electronics if you encounter a spill, can save you time, money, and salvage your data.

Founded by Reuben Zielinski and Joel Trusty in Indianapolis in 2013, Redux uses their patented technique to remove moisture from liquid-damaged cell phones, cameras, tablets, MP3 players and other devices, and returns them to working condition.

The idea for Redux came when Trusty’s wife subjected her cell phone to a full cycle in the washing machine and was devastated that not only was the phone ruined, but all the data, contact information, photos and videos were lost. With a background in manufacturing, Trusty thought of a concept for how to save his wife’s phone and knew that his friend and neighbor, Zielinski, an ex-IBM employee, had the equipment to put it into practice. Together, they fixed the phone, and within one week created the first Redux model.

Currently, Redux is available at over 2000 wireless retail stores throughout the U.S. For more information, visit the Redux website.

company_summary:
Clogged up dryer vents can lead to dryer fires. LintFree Dryer Vent Cleaning service can professionally clean your dryer vents quickly, efficiently and affordably. 

While dryer lint traps typically catch about 60% of clothing lint, the other 40% ends up in the dryer vent system. Over time, it builds up in areas not seen by the naked eye. Accumulated lint in the dryer vent system, combined with very hot air trying to get through the system, could result in unexpected and hard-to-find fires. Don’t be one of 18,000 people to experience a dryer vent fire this year! 

Serving the Denver metro area, LintFree Dryer Vent Cleaning is owned and operated by Stuart Urban, a Certified Dryer Exhaust Technician. Certified through the Chimney Safety Institute of America, Stuart has been servicing area dryer vents since 2010, checking for problems before doing any work, providing upfront pricing so you know exactly what you’re paying for, and asking if you’re completely satisfied before leaving. Stuart takes dryer vent cleaning seriously and is ready to get the job done for you.

profile_summary: "All the worlds a stage, and all the men and women merely players" - William Shakespeare Performing lit a fire within me. I love telling a good story. Whether I'm acting in a short film or selling a product or service, there is a story to be told. The beginning always starts with a person with a problem, followed by attempts to escape it, failures to do so, then finally discovering a solution that works. No matter where I enter, my goal is to help clients see themselves as the hero of their own story. Framing myself or the brand not as the savior, but as the guide or tool that leads them to their own victory. I started my sales journey in the roofing industry. My father started and owns a successful roofing company in Dallas, TX where I started working almost immediately after graduating college with an oh-so-versatile Fine-Arts Acting degree. I am incredibly proud of this degree, as it was a highly competitive program for which thousands applied and only 14 were accepted. I quickly found that the communication skills, collaborative training, and performance techniques I learned in acting classes were paramount to any product knowledge I had mastered from being raised in the industry. At the end of the day, people want to be heard and valued. Listening is one of the most important aspects of performance, and I soon found the same to be true of sales. In order to form a solution, it's crucial to understand the problem. It's typical for actors and performers to live and think abstractly, but my approach to performance has been one of deep analytics. I can sometimes become obsessed with the question, "why?" I avoid the pits of existential crisis this thinking can lead to, and rather use it as a means to understand motivation. Why do people do what they do? The human condition has always fascinated me, it is as surprising as it is elusive. Being human myself, completely understanding people is impossible, which makes it just that much more fun to try to figure out. It's a true journey, and I've learned to delight in the process.

company_summary:
Hestan Vineyards is a 41-acre vineyard located on Napa Valley's eastern slopes at the base of Okell Hill. Individually farmed blocks planted to all five Bordeaux varietals--Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc and Malbec--blanket the rolling hills overlooking the vineyard's 11-acre pond.

Consulting winemaker Mark Herold began working with the Hestan estate in 2002, creating his hallmark opulent wines with the 2002 Hestan Cabernet Sauvignon and the 2002 Meyer Cabernet Sauvignon. Both were a critical success.

In 2005, winemaker Jeff Gaffner began the Stephanie project to showcase all five of the Bordeaux varietals planted at the estate. He produces a Stephane Red Wine, (Bordeaux blend) , Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon from the property.

This harvest (2009) Thomas Rivers Brown is taking over production of the Hestan wines from Herold. Considered one of the rising stars of Napa Valley, Thomas made history as the youngest winemaker to receive two perfect 100 point scores from Robert Parker.

Hestan Vineyards owner Stanley Cheng notes, "We feel very excited and privileged to have winemakers of such accomplishment interpret our vineyard's terroir in such wonderfully expressive styles. We think our entire portfolio of wines--Hestan, Meyer and Stephanie--will continue to improve with each vintage."

company_summary:
Clogged up dryer vents can lead to dryer fires. LintFree Dryer Vent Cleaning service can professionally clean your dryer vents quickly, efficiently and affordably. 

While dryer lint traps typically catch about 60% of clothing lint, the other 40% ends up in the dryer vent system. Over time, it builds up in areas not seen by the naked eye. Accumulated lint in the dryer vent system, combined with very hot air trying to get through the system, could result in unexpected and hard-to-find fires. Don’t be one of 18,000 people to experience a dryer vent fire this year! 

Serving the Denver metro area, LintFree Dryer Vent Cleaning is owned and operated by Stuart Urban, a Certified Dryer Exhaust Technician. Certified through the Chimney Safety Institute of America, Stuart has been servicing area dryer vents since 2010, checking for problems before doing any work, providing upfront pricing so you know exactly what you’re paying for, and asking if you’re completely satisfied before leaving. Stuart takes dryer vent cleaning seriously and is ready to get the job done for you.

Started in 2006, Advanced Component Services (ACS) is a contract manufacturer, commercial component distributor, e-commerce third party seller and, since 2014, an Amazon Vendor. ACS operates its main office and distribution center in the Seattle area and has support offices and warehouses in China and England.

Commercial Division

From its inception, ACS has operated as a contract manufacturer and commercial distributor primarily focused on developing and selling components used in commercial food service equipment, beverage equipment and lighting.

ACS has manufactured over 4,000 different products for its commercial customers. We directly or indirectly supply components to companies operating supermarkets, c-stores, bakeries, coffee shops, restaurants, office coffee service businesses and other types of establishments.

In addition to the products manufactured by our company, we maintain a large inventory of popular components from companies such as Robertshaw, Parker, Southco, Oetiker and several other fully stocked lines.

ACS is constantly looking for new products to manufacture for our existing and new commercial customers.

E-Commerce Division

In 2014, ACS launched an e-commerce division as part of a strategic initiative to create new revenue streams for its products. The e-commerce division has been very successful and has generated triple-digit growth since its creation. At the end of its fourth year, the e-commerce division was actively marketing and selling tens of thousands of products through a flow of up to 20,000 orders each month.

To support its e-commerce division’s growth, ACS is actively seeking new partnerships with component manufacturers and authorized distribution companies to purchase as many new products as possible to add to its product offering.

The e-commerce division’s products are sold through all the major marketplaces and standalone websites.
